description:
You are an MLOps engineer who must create a **deterministic backup** of the current experiment artifacts so they can be promoted to long-term storage.  
Everything to be archived is already located under  

    /home/user/experiments

and that directory tree must be compressed into a single file named  

    /home/user/backups/backup_20240101_000000.tar.gz

After creating the archive you must also write a one-shot log that documents the backup.  
Write the log to  

    /home/user/backup_logs/backup_20240101_000000.log

The log **must use exactly the four-section layout shown below, with no extra blank lines or additional fields** (the automated tests depend on this strict formatting).

---------  REQUIRED LOG FORMAT  ---------
ARCHIVE=/home/user/backups/backup_20240101_000000.tar.gz
SIZE_BYTES=<integer size of the archive file in bytes>
MD5=<32-character lowercase hexadecimal checksum of the archive>
FILES:
<one entry per archived file, relative to /home/user, sorted alphabetically>
-----------------------------------------

Example (the numbers below are only illustrative—your actual SIZE_BYTES and MD5 will differ):

ARCHIVE=/home/user/backups/backup_20240101_000000.tar.gz  
SIZE_BYTES=12345  
MD5=9e107d9d372bb6826bd81d3542a419d6  
FILES:  
experiments/run_001/metrics.json  
experiments/run_001/model.pkl  
experiments/run_002/metrics.json  
experiments/run_002/model.pkl  

Your task is finished when:
1. /home/user/backups/backup_20240101_000000.tar.gz exists and contains an exact replica of the entire /home/user/experiments directory tree (preserving internal paths).
2. /home/user/backup_logs/backup_20240101_000000.log exists and follows the format above.
3. The list under the FILES: section is alphabetically sorted and matches the actual files inside the archive.

Hint: Only 2–3 standard shell commands are necessary (one to make sure destination directories exist, one to create the archive, one to compute checksum & write the log). Feel free to pipe or redirect as needed, but do not use super-user privileges.
truth:
Initial filesystem (already present when the agent starts):

Directory: /home/user/experiments
 ├─ run_001
 │   ├─ model.pkl      (ASCII text — contents: "run_001 model checkpoint\n")
 │   └─ metrics.json   (ASCII text — contents: '{"accuracy": 0.91, "loss": 0.23}\n')
 └─ run_002
     ├─ model.pkl      (ASCII text — contents: "run_002 model checkpoint\n")
     └─ metrics.json   (ASCII text — contents: '{"accuracy": 0.93, "loss": 0.19}\n')

Empty directories that already exist (writable by user):
  /home/user/backups
  /home/user/backup_logs

Expected FILES list (alphabetically, relative to /home/user):

experiments/run_001/metrics.json  
experiments/run_001/model.pkl  
experiments/run_002/metrics.json  
experiments/run_002/model.pkl  

Automated verification steps:

1. Confirm /home/user/backups/backup_20240101_000000.tar.gz exists.
2. Untar the archive in a temp directory and ensure the four files above exist with byte-for-byte identical content to originals.
3. Compute its byte size and MD5 checksum; compare both values against those recorded in /home/user/backup_logs/backup_20240101_000000.log.
4. Ensure log format exactly matches specification (first three keyed lines, the literal FILES: line, then exactly four file entries in the correct order, no duplicates or extra whitespace).
name: task_000000_d5588849
